response.justification: The candidate has an extensive software development career spanning 15+ years, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The most critical disqualifier is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is listed as a mandatory requirement. Beyond that, the candidate's background is almost entirely in web, full-stack, and data infrastructure work — there is no evidence of security domain expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal), embedded networking, Linux kernel modification, or LAN-switch product experience. C programming appears only briefly in early career entries and an academic project, and there is no mention of Layer-2/Layer-3 networking, switch chip knowledge, or BIOS/bootloader work.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, embedded, and data domains demonstrates strong technical adaptability
Some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and in an academic wave field synthesis project, showing baseline familiarity with lower-level systems
Demonstrated engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and code quality practices align well with collaborative embedded development workflows
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency mentioned anywhere — this is a hard mandatory requirement and alone disqualifies the candidate
Zero security domain experience: no evidence of work with MACsec, 802.1X, captive portals, encryption protocols, or device attack/tamper detection
No networking fundamentals depth: no Layer-2/Layer-3, switch operation, NETCONF, SNMP, or embedded firmware experience visible in the resume

key_factors.factor: No security or networking domain knowledge
